title: Case Taxonomy
description: The canonical, versioned classification vocabulary for the evaluation corpus. Referenced by 00_case_envelope; validated at ingest. THE CASE_ID IS NOT PART OF THIS CONTRACT — it is an opaque partition key; case_metadata is authoritative (see id_rule).

taxonomy_version.description: Semver. A taxonomy change is a version bump + re-checksum, NEVER a schema migration — the whole reason this is a dataset.

id_rule.description: The demotion rule, carried as data so it travels with the taxonomy rather than living only in prose someone can miss.

distribution_rule.description: Operator ruling 2026-07-16: the difficulty mix is a GUIDE, not a gate. Carried here because the strict reading previously calcified out of planning artifacts.

axes.description: The axes a category_tag may classify on. Their existence is the point: tranche 1's categories are not one taxonomy, and pretending they are would make coverage across any of them meaningless.

specialty.description: Specialty codes. A SUPERSET: tranche 2's 60 clinical specialties ∪ legacy codes still carried by attested cases.

code.description: Up to 8 chars — deliberately WIDER than the case_id's [A-Z]{2,6}. OPHTHAL (7) is exactly why: the code must not be constrained by an identifier slot it is not stored in.

legacy.description: true = present in the pre-taxonomy 00_case_envelope enum and carried by attested cases. Load-bearing: removing or renaming one invalidates clinician-attested cases.

legacy_only.description: true = legacy with NO tranche-2 equivalent (e.g. MSK, which tranche 2 splits across ORTHO/SPORTS/RHEUM/PAIN/REHAB). Kept because attested cases carry it, not because the taxonomy wants it.

id_prefixes.description: Recorded ONLY where the id segment cannot equal the code. Exists so a reader who greps SPEC-OPHTH lands somewhere that explains why — NOT so anyone may resume deriving meaning from an id.

category_tags.description: Tranche 1's corpus categories. Each declares its axis, because they are not mutually comparable: Cardiovascular is an organ system, Geriatric & Frailty is a cohort, DEI is an axis of inequity.

ingested.description: true = a batch already in data/cases/. Its code is the operator's, recorded in the batch history — load-bearing, not chosen here.

expansion_unconfirmed.description: true = the display name is INFERRED from source-corpus naming and content, and awaits operator confirmation. Flagged rather than asserted.

difficulty_tier.description: The EXISTING 7-value enum, mapped not replaced.

id_digit.description: DESCRIPTIVE ONLY — the historical id correspondence. case_metadata.difficulty_tier is authoritative; a re-rated case is NOT renamed.

records_checksum.description: sha256 over {specialty, category_tags, difficulty_tier}, computed with the repo's own checksumRecords (imported, never re-implemented — a second implementation is a second answer).
